note: Adding domain= parameter support to {% trans %} tag: modifying InternationalizationExtension.parse() (line ~354), _make_node() (line ~509), and _install_callables() (line ~315) to support dgettext/dngettext with a domain param

type: SURVEY

type: CLAIM

type: CLAIM

note: I'll own ext.py since both our changes touch parse() and _make_node() directly. Please send a SPEC for the domain= feature (exact parsing rules, error messages, _install_callables changes, node construction) and I'll implement both features together to avoid clobbering.

type: DEFER
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Conflicting claims - deferring to you as owner of src/jinja2/ext.py. Implementing my metadata feature per the SPEC below.

type: CLAIM
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: We both claimed simultaneously - resolving by agent id order (agent1 owns). I will own src/jinja2/ext.py and implement both features. Please send your SPEC now (param syntax, validation, error messages, node attrs, _install_callables changes) for the metadata feature and DEFER the file to me.

type: SPEC
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
spec: Feature: optional metadata params (note, created, modified) on {% trans %} tag.

1) In InternationalizationExtension.parse(), inside the while-loop that parses tag parameters (currently: while parser.stream.current.type != 'block_end': ...), before the block_end token is reached, add handling so that when token.value is one of 'note', 'created', 'modified' (checked BEFORE the generic 'trimmed'/'notrimmed' handling, using an elif branch), it consumes an assignment and stores a string literal into a new dict:
   metadata: t.Dict[str, str] = {}  (initialize near variables/trimmed at top of parse())

   Detection logic (as an elif branch alongside the existing 'if parser.stream.current.type == "assign":' check): actually the cleanest integration point is: right after 'token = parser.stream.expect("name")' and the duplicate-variable check, BEFORE the existing 'if parser.stream.current.type == "assign":' block, add:
     if token.value in ('note', 'created', 'modified'):
         if parser.stream.current.type != 'assign':
             parser.fail(f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} requires a value assignment.", token.lineno, exc=TemplateAssertionError)
         next(parser.stream)
         value_token = parser.stream.current
         if value_token.type != 'string':
             parser.fail(f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} must be a string literal.", token.lineno, exc=TemplateAssertionError)
         metadata[token.value] = parser.parse_expression().value
         continue
   (parser.parse_expression() on a string token yields a nodes.Const; .value is the python str)
   This must run BEFORE the existing 'elif trimmed is None and token.value in ("trimmed", "notrimmed"):' branch and before the final else that treats token.value as a plain variable name — i.e. metadata names must never fall into the variables dict or become a Name-load variable, and must not affect plural_expr/num_called_num detection. They can appear in any order relative to trimmed/variables/pluralization.
   Also: metadata param names ('note','created','modified') should NOT trigger the 'translatable variable defined twice' check that uses the  dict (they use a separate  dict, so no collision there) — but do still guard against duplicate metadata keys not being required (not specified, fine either way, duplicates just overwrite).

2) Pass  through to self._make_node(...) call at the end of parse() (add as a new argument).

3) In _make_node(), add parameter  to the signature. At the very end of the method, right before , build the output_node first:
     output_node = nodes.Output([node])
     if metadata:
         output_node.trans_metadata = metadata
     return output_node
   Nodes without metadata must NOT have the trans_metadata attribute at all (don't set it to {} or None — just skip assignment). Must be accessible via hasattr(node, 'trans_metadata') and direct attribute access.

4) No changes needed to babel_extract or _install_callables for this feature. Rendering output must be byte-identical whether metadata is present or not (metadata is purely a parse-time AST annotation, never touched at runtime/compile in CodeGenerator).

Error messages must match exactly:
 - "metadata parameter '{name}' must be a string literal."
 - "metadata parameter '{name}' requires a value assignment."
(use f-string with token.value!r which renders as 'name' with single quotes, matching the required format)

Examples that must parse correctly:
 {% trans note="Context for translators" %}Hello World{% endtrans %}
 {% trans note="User count", created="2024-01-01" %}{{ count }} user{% pluralize %}{{ count }} users{% endtrans %}
 {% trans user_count, note="Message with variable" %}{{ user_count }} item{% endtrans %}
 {% trans note="Trimmed message", trimmed %}  Hello  World  {% endtrans %}
 {% trans note="test", user_count, trimmed %}...{% endtrans %} (metadata can appear before/after/interspersed with variables and trimmed)

type: SPEC
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
spec: Domain feature spec: Add optional domain="literal string" parameter to {% trans %} tag, parsed alongside existing params (e.g. {% trans domain="myapp" %} or {% trans user=user.name, domain="myapp" %}). In InternationalizationExtension.parse(): after parsing the existing var-name-to-expr mapping loop (the while parser.stream.skip_if('comma') block), detect a 'domain' identifier followed by '=' followed by a string literal token; store its value as a python str called  (default None if absent). If 'domain' appears more than once, call self._parse_block or raise self.fail/parser.fail with a message containing 'domain' and 'twice' (raise TemplateAssertionError). If the value after domain= is not a constant string literal (e.g. a Name node), raise TemplateAssertionError with message containing 'domain', 'string', and 'literal'. domain= may appear interspersed with other var=expr params, in any order, comma-separated same as existing params - so extend the existing parsing loop rather than requiring it in a fixed position. Pass  through to self._parse_block(...) and then to self._make_node(..., domain=domain_name). In _make_node(): when domain is not None, build the call node using  (2-arg: domain, message) instead of  (1-arg: message) for singular-only case; use  (4-arg: domain, singular, plural, n) instead of  (3-arg: singular, plural, n) for plural case. domain is passed as nodes.Const(domain) - a constant string node, first argument. When domain is None, behavior is 100% unchanged (existing gettext/ngettext calls). In _install_callables(): ensure 'dgettext' and 'dngettext' names are available as environment globals alongside existing 'gettext'/'ngettext'/'_' - i.e. add wrapper functions dgettext(domain, message) and dngettext(domain, singular, plural, n) that call through to the underlying translations object's dgettext/dngettext methods (falling back gracefully for old-style gettext modules that may lack dgettext, similar to how existing code handles missing ngettext on old-style translations - e.g. wrap in try/except AttributeError or getattr with a fallback to plain gettext/ngettext ignoring domain). These new globals must not break existing 'gettext'/'ngettext' entries or the newstyle/oldstyle detection logic already in _install_callables. All existing trans tag features (pluralization, variables, trimmed, notrimmed) must continue to work unchanged when domain is absent, and must also work in combination with domain when present (e.g. {% trans domain="myapp", count=n %}...{% pluralize %}...{% endtrans %}).
note: This is my domain-support spec; please implement alongside your metadata feature in ext.py

type: SPEC
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
spec: Domain feature spec: Add optional domain="literal string" parameter to {% trans %} tag, parsed alongside existing params (e.g. {% trans domain="myapp" %} or {% trans user=user.name, domain="myapp" %}).

In InternationalizationExtension.parse(): after parsing the existing var-name-to-expr mapping loop (the while parser.stream.skip_if('comma') block), detect a 'domain' identifier followed by '=' followed by a string literal token; store its value as a python str called domain_name (default None if absent). If 'domain' appears more than once, raise TemplateAssertionError (via self.fail / parser.fail) with a message containing 'domain' and 'twice'. If the value after domain= is not a constant string literal (e.g. a Name node), raise TemplateAssertionError with message containing 'domain', 'string', and 'literal'. domain= may appear interspersed with other var=expr params, in any order, comma-separated same as existing params - so extend the existing parsing loop rather than requiring it in a fixed position. Pass domain_name through to self._parse_block(...) and then to self._make_node(..., domain=domain_name).

In _make_node(): when domain is not None, build the call node using dgettext (2-arg: domain, message) instead of gettext (1-arg: message) for singular-only case; use dngettext (4-arg: domain, singular, plural, n) instead of ngettext (3-arg: singular, plural, n) for plural case. domain is passed as nodes.Const(domain) - a constant string node, first argument. When domain is None, behavior is 100% unchanged (existing gettext/ngettext calls).

In _install_callables(): ensure 'dgettext' and 'dngettext' names are available as environment globals alongside existing 'gettext'/'ngettext'/'_' - i.e. add wrapper functions dgettext(domain, message) and dngettext(domain, singular, plural, n) that call through to the underlying translations object's dgettext/dngettext methods (falling back gracefully for old-style gettext modules that may lack dgettext, similar to how existing code handles missing ngettext on old-style translations - e.g. wrap in try/except AttributeError or getattr with a fallback to plain gettext/ngettext ignoring domain). These new globals must not break existing 'gettext'/'ngettext' entries or the newstyle/oldstyle detection logic already in _install_callables.

All existing trans tag features (pluralization, variables, trimmed, notrimmed) must continue to work unchanged when domain is absent, and must also work in combination with domain when present (e.g. {% trans domain="myapp", count=n %}...{% pluralize %}...{% endtrans %}).
note: This is my domain-support spec; please implement alongside your metadata feature in ext.py

type: CLAIM
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Acknowledged your metadata SPEC (note/created/modified params, trans_metadata attr on Output node). I own src/jinja2/ext.py and will implement both features now.

type: SPEC
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
spec: Feature: optional metadata params (note, created, modified) on {% trans %} tag.

1) In InternationalizationExtension.parse(), inside the while-loop that parses tag parameters (currently: "while parser.stream.current.type != 'block_end':" ...), add handling so that when token.value is one of 'note', 'created', 'modified', it consumes an assignment and stores a string literal into a new dict:
   metadata: t.Dict[str, str] = {}  (initialize near variables/trimmed at top of parse(), before the while loop)

   Integration point: right after "token = parser.stream.expect('name')" and the duplicate-variable check, BEFORE the existing "if parser.stream.current.type == 'assign':" block, add a new branch:

     if token.value in ('note', 'created', 'modified'):
         if parser.stream.current.type != 'assign':
             parser.fail(f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} requires a value assignment.", token.lineno, exc=TemplateAssertionError)
         next(parser.stream)
         if parser.stream.current.type != 'string':
             parser.fail(f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} must be a string literal.", token.lineno, exc=TemplateAssertionError)
         metadata[token.value] = parser.parse_expression().value
         continue

   (parser.parse_expression() on a string token yields a nodes.Const; .value is the python str)

   This must run BEFORE the existing "elif trimmed is None and token.value in ('trimmed', 'notrimmed'):" branch and before the final else that treats token.value as a plain variable name -- i.e. metadata names must never fall into the `variables` dict or become a Name-load variable, and must not affect plural_expr/num_called_num detection. They can appear in any order relative to trimmed/variables/pluralization (e.g. "note=X, user_count, trimmed" or "user_count, note=X, trimmed" all valid).

   Metadata params use a separate `metadata` dict, so they never collide with the existing "translatable variable ... defined twice" check on `variables`.

2) Pass `metadata` through to the self._make_node(...) call at the end of parse() (add as a new argument, e.g. after num_called_num).

3) In _make_node(), add parameter `metadata: t.Dict[str, str]` to the signature. At the very end of the method, replace "return nodes.Output([node])" with:

     output_node = nodes.Output([node])
     if metadata:
         output_node.trans_metadata = metadata
     return output_node

   Nodes without metadata must NOT have the trans_metadata attribute at all (don't set it to {} or None -- just skip the assignment entirely). Must be accessible via hasattr(node, 'trans_metadata') and direct attribute access when present.

4) No changes needed to babel_extract or _install_callables for this feature. Rendering output must be byte-identical whether metadata is present or not (metadata is purely a parse-time AST annotation, never touched at runtime or in CodeGenerator).

Error messages must match exactly (note the single-quoted param name from !r):
 - "metadata parameter 'note' must be a string literal."
 - "metadata parameter 'note' requires a value assignment."
(substitute 'created'/'modified' as appropriate; use f-string with token.value!r)

Examples that must parse correctly:
 {% trans note="Context for translators" %}Hello World{% endtrans %}
 {% trans note="User count", created="2024-01-01" %}{{ count }} user{% pluralize %}{{ count }} users{% endtrans %}
 {% trans user_count, note="Message with variable" %}{{ user_count }} item{% endtrans %}
 {% trans note="Trimmed message", trimmed %}  Hello  World  {% endtrans %}
 {% trans note="test", user_count, trimmed %}...{% endtrans %} (metadata can appear before/after/interspersed with variables and trimmed)

Examples that must raise TemplateAssertionError:
 {% trans note=some_var %}Hello{% endtrans %}  -> "metadata parameter 'note' must be a string literal."
 {% trans note %}Hello{% endtrans %}  -> "metadata parameter 'note' requires a value assignment."
